title: Madikeri Fort
tagline: Madikeri Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Madikeri Fort, also called Mercara Fort, is a fort in Madikeri, in the Kodagu district of the Indian state of Karnataka, first built by Mudduraja, a banajigara king of lingayath fa
overview.description: Madikeri Fort, also called Mercara Fort, is a fort in Madikeri, in the Kodagu district of the Indian state of Karnataka, first built by Mudduraja, a banajigara king of lingayath faith, who was from a family of bedanur royal house, in the second half of the 17th century. Mudduraja also built the palace within the fort. It was rebuilt and restructured in granite by Tipu Sultan, and the site was then renamed Jaffarabad. Madikeri Fort is one of the many forts built or rebuilt by Tipu Sultan during his reign in the second half of the 18th century.

topPlaces.name: St. Mark's Church, Mercara
topPlaces.description: The St. Mark's Church, is located within the Mercara Fort, Coorg, India and was raised in 1859, by the officers and men of the East India Company.

topPlaces.name: Coorg State
topPlaces.description: Coorg State was a Part-C state in India which existed from 1950 to 1956. When the Constitution of India came into force on 26 January 1950, most of the existing provinces were reconstituted into states.

topPlaces.name: Madikeri
topPlaces.description: Madikeri (Kodava: [mɐɖikeːri]), earlier called as Mercara is city and headquarters of Kodagu district in the Karnataka state of India. It is recognised as one of the world's eight "hottest hotspots" of biological diversity and is also a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

topPlaces.name: Raja's Seat
topPlaces.description: Raja's Seat (Seat of the King) is a culturally significant site and tourist attraction in Madikeri of Coorg District. It is 270km away from Bangalore, the capital of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Abbey Falls
topPlaces.description: Abbey Falls (also spelled Abbi Falls and Abbe Falls) is a waterfall in Kodagu, in the Western Ghats of Karnataka, India. It is located 8 km from the Madikeri, 122 km from Mysore, 144 km from Mangalore and 268 km from Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Kodagu
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya is a school in Kodagu, India, and is a part of the Navodaya Vidyalaya group. The school is located near Madikeri, which is 7 km from Madikeri, the headquarters of Kodagu district.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — St. Mark's Church, Mercara & around

items.note: The St. Mark's Church, is located within the Mercara Fort, Coorg, India and was raised in 1859, by the officers and men of the East India Company.

Coorg State was a Part-C state in India which existed from 1950 to 1956. When the Constitution of India came into force on 26 January 1950, most of the existing provinces were reconstituted into states.

Madikeri (Kodava: [mɐɖikeːri]), earlier called as Mercara is city and headquarters of Kodagu district in the Karnataka state of India. It is recognised as one of the world's eight "hottest hotspots" of biological diversity and is also a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Raja's Seat (Seat of the King) is a culturally significant site and tourist attraction in Madikeri of Coorg District. It is 270km away from Bangalore, the capital of Karnataka.

Abbey Falls (also spelled Abbi Falls and Abbe Falls) is a waterfall in Kodagu, in the Western Ghats of Karnataka, India. It is located 8 km from the Madikeri, 122 km from Mysore, 144 km from Mangalore and 268 km from Bangalore.

Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya is a school in Kodagu, India, and is a part of the Navodaya Vidyalaya group. The school is located near Madikeri, which is 7 km from Madikeri, the headquarters of Kodagu district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Madikeri Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Madikeri Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Madikeri Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (102 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (99 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Madikeri Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Madikeri Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Madikeri Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Madikeri Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Madikeri Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are St. Mark's Church, Mercara, Coorg State, Madikeri, Raja's Seat, Abbey Falls.
